stance.name: Trading System Theta + Jewel
stance.boundary: Paper/research only. No real-money orders without exact explicit approval.
rules: Prefer QQQ, NVDA, SPY, and DIA when live setup quality is comparable; AMD and TSLA are allowed but lower priority.
Review-only symbols may be staged for observation but should not be treated as priority paper trades.
Avoid symbols with negative avgR until a later backtest changes the grade.
Block active day-trade staging within 48 hours of lunar perigee/apogee under the high-win paper profile.
Use 1R as the first target; treat 1.5R+ as optional runner research after first profit is earned.

tradeHistory.notes: Jewel training allowed; win 54.3%; avgR 0.09; 1.5R reach 16.2%. Aggressive paper mode: Price is above 120 EMA/VWAP and close enough to a Square-of-Nine boundary; stage continuation only on break of recent high.

recent: Memory: 168 trades, 19 skips, 51.2% wins, 5.12R total, 0.030R avg.
Promotion status: not promoted; keep as research/advisory memory.
cycleRules: Highest focus requires preferred/allowed/lunar-tighten training, trend agreement, SO9 distance <= 0.75 ATR, and compression near a boundary.
Review-only and avoid grades do not become active trades from pattern recognition alone.
Lunar context is recorded for study, but lunar-tagged setups require stronger confirmation because current training shows lunar underperformance.
Track whether recurring pivot cycles cluster before successful paper entries; do not trade cycle rhythm without price and risk confirmation.

stockLive.privacyNote: Live stock chart uses TradingView public chart embeds for the locked Theta stock universe only. Private account balances, orders, API keys, and secrets are never sent to the browser.

missingGaps: TradingView confirmation is still usually recorded as not-recorded in the learning ledger.
The historical backtest notes still say the proprietary blue/white intraday schedule is missing.
Screenshots or chart snapshots around entries and exits would improve post-trade labeling.
Manual tags for why a setup was accepted or rejected would help separate good losses from rule mistakes.
howToHelp: Send screenshots at entry, stop/target hit, and the next scheduled Theta signal.
Record the exact local time, symbol, timeframe, direction, entry, stop, target, and whether it was paper or real observation.
Tag whether the setup matched the 120 EMA, Jewel SO9 boundary, lunar window, compression, and price-action confirmation rules.
Flag any trade you think should not have been taken so it can be added to repeated-loss family research.
Keep real-money decisions separate until paper stats clearly show wins exceeding losses and risk is explicitly approved.
